title: quercus setulosa subsp. setulosa
intro: Quercus setulosa subsp. setulosa, also known as Setulosa Oak, is a subspecies of tree belonging to the Quercus genus, characterized by its distinctive hairy leaves and small, acorn-like fruits. The medicinal properties of Setulosa Oak lie in its bark, which contains tannins and other bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits associated with Setulosa Oak include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress. In traditional herbal preparations, Setulosa Oak bark is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall well-being and alleviate specific health concerns.
